Title: Inland River Towboat
Awardee: TAI HOLDINGS, INC Pensacola FL 32507 USA
Description: Contract was awarded on the basis of Best Value for a new construction vessel that was already in production, with acceptance in November 2026. Commercial off the shelf (COTS) design is being used with minor modifications to meet Government requirements (e.g. fuel, oil and potable water tank segregation; additional insulation and heaters added). Steiner Hull 1081 (USACE Name TBD) will be 100 ft long, 34 ft beam (width), draft of 8 ft 6 in, powered by 3 x 800 HP Tier III S6R2 Mitsubishi Diesel engines. Vessel with have berths for up to 8 crew and two 65 ton barge winches. Vessel will be used by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers - St. Paul District - Maintenance & Repair Section to move barges, floating plants and other equipment used in the repair and mainenance of locks, dams, and other Government assets. Vessel is being built by Steiner Construction Company Inc., 9195 Little River Road; Bayou La Batre, AL 36509

Title: Coconino Portal Sign Installation
Awardee: BDS SOLUTIONS LIMITED LIABILITY CO Clarkson KY 42726 USA
Description: This project will consist of the removal and disposal of concrete and rock masonry portal sign bases in clear zones, the removal and disposal of existing wood portal signs, construction of new portal sign bases, and installation of Government-Furnished signs at 8 different sites across the Coconino National Forest, three (3) on the Flagstaff Ranger District, three (3) on the Mogollon Rim Ranger District, and two (2) on the Red Rock Ranger District. This project also includes optional work items for the removal and disposal of concrete and rock masonry portal sign bases at five (5) additional sites; the removal and disposal of a wood post base at one (1) additional site; the removal of existing wood portal signs at a total of ten (10) sites, four (4) additional sites; the installation of steel, square posts and aluminum signs at four (4) sites; and the installation of wooden portal signs at four (4) sites. Coordination with various government agencies will be required.

Title: Cargo Nets (Repair)
Awardee: TAC INDUSTRIES INC Springfield OH 45505 USA
Description: This synopsis is to announce the award of a contract for the service of repairing HCU-7A/E low profile side nets (NSN: 1670-0l-603-0034CT) and HCU-15/C top nets (NSN: 1670-00-969-4103CT).The HCU-7A/E and HCU-15/C nets are designed to meet the specifications of MIL-DTL-27444. They are used as a complete system to secure palletized cargo on HCU-6/E 463L pallets. These cargo nets are utilized by the U.S. Air Force, across the Department of Defense, and by allied partners. They are critical for air and ground transport operations on the following platforms: Aircraft: C-5, C-17, C-130, KC-10, KC-135 Cargo Loaders: Tunner, Halvorsen The nets are supplied by TAC Industries Inc, as designated under the Ability One Program. The central nonprofit agency facilitating this contract is Source America®. The Committee for Purchase From People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled has the sole authority to determine the fair market price and conducts an administrative review of the final negotiated price for approval. This procurement was conducted in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8, as the required supplies are on the Procurement List maintained by the Committee for Purchase From People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled. This makes the Ability One Program a mandatory Government source. The acquisition is executed under the authority of Public Law 92-28.The proposed service is being performed at TAC's facility in Springfield, Ohio.

Title: SBIR Phase II TACFI -Super Jammer Vehicle Arm Integration-
Description: Small Business Innovation Research Program Phase II Action Full and Open Competition after exclusion of sources This effort will modernize the 7,000?pound munitions lift truck by advancing the SuperJammer arm and integrating it onto a prototype SuperJammer vehicle, building on insights from SBIR Phase II contract FA864923P1050. The work includes refining arm compatibility with aircraft loading adapters, incorporating user feedback to optimize system integration, and developing a prototype vehicle for assessment by Air Force stakeholders. The integrated system will undergo demonstration and performance evaluation in a relevant operational environment to validate functionality, confirm load?handling capability, and ensure alignment with mission requirements.

Title: Charleston Pier Mike Recapitalization, USCG Base Charleston, South Carolina
Awardee: WHITING-TURNER CONTRACTING COMPANY, THE Towson MD 21286 USA
Description: Design/Build for Charleston Pier Mike Recapitalization, USCG Base Charleston, South Carolina includes the complete demolition of the existing Pier Mike and all associated waterfront and landside structures, utilities, and supporting facilities. It includes construction of a new Pier Mike capable of homeporting four single cutters and one nested visiting cutter in the mooring configuration as shown on RFP drawings. The USCG will designate Pier Mike for homeporting of the Offshore Patrol Cutters (OPC) classes, however, each homeported and visiting berth must be capable of alternately mooring National Security Cutters (NSC).

Title: Cargo Nets (Production)
Awardee: TAC INDUSTRIES INC Springfield OH 45505 USA
Description: This synopsis is to announce the award of a contract for the procurement of HCU-7A/E low profile side nets (NSN: 1670-0l-603-0034CT) and HCU-15/C top nets (NSN: 1670-00-969-4103CT). The HCU-7A/E and HCU-15/C nets are designed to meet the specifications of MIL-DTL-27444. They are used as a complete system to secure palletized cargo on HCU-6/E 463L pallets. These cargo nets are utilized by the U.S. Air Force, across the Department of Defense, and by allied partners. They are critical for air and ground transport operations on the following platforms: Aircraft: C-5, C-17, C-130, KC-10, KC-135 Cargo Loaders: Tunner, Halvorsen The nets are supplied by TAC Industries Inc, as designated under the Ability One Program. The central nonprofit agency facilitating this contract is Source America®. The Committee for Purchase From People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled has the sole authority to determine the fair market price and conducts an administrative review of the final negotiated price for approval. This procurement was conducted in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation (FAR) Part 8, as the required supplies are on the Procurement List maintained by the Committee for Purchase From People Who Are Blind or Severely Disabled. This makes the Ability One Program a mandatory Government source. The acquisition is executed under the authority of Public Law 92-28.
